knownFacts: Lead notes include: hvac, 3.7 rating, 3 Google reviews, 5 W 32nd St 3rd floor, New York, NY 10001, USA
missingData: No license, warranty, emergency, same-day, or years-in-business claims
designRead.positioning: A premium editorial single-page site that helps Manhattan visitors make a direct HVAC call with clear context and no inflated claims.
antiGenericRules: Do not invent awards, guarantees, licensing, response times, or emergency availability
Do not make a centered SaaS hero plus three cards
Use request architecture and buyer guidance instead of fake proof

colourScheme.concept: Warm editorial paper balanced by Manhattan steel and copper mechanical accents.
usage: Steel-dark for final CTA and footer contrast
Copper used sparingly for active states and primary CTAs
Utility green-grey for technical panels and diagram fields

typography.strategy: Editorial display face paired with precise readable UI/body face.
rules: H1 should land in 2 to 3 desktop lines
Use short labels, not SECTION 01 style meta labels
Do not use Arial, Helvetica, or browser default font as the identity
Keep letter spacing at 0 except tiny positive tracking for short utility labels if needed

imports.name: Fraunces
imports.usage: Hero, section statements, final CTA headline

customFonts.fallback: If Google font access fails during build, keep the variables but use a carefully declared serif/sans fallback stack as backup.

assetPlan.photos: No supplied photos and stock assets are not allowed.
assetPlan.logo: Use a typographic wordmark for New York HVAC NYC.
assetPlan.primaryVisual: CSS-only Manhattan mechanical map panel with grid lines, airflow arrows, building blocks, and contact callouts.

sections.purpose: Premium navigation with real anchors and visible call CTA.
Art-directed first viewport with service, area, and call path.
content: Headline: Manhattan HVAC calls, routed with clear next steps.
Short deck grounded in supplied business data

sections.purpose: Compact factual context instead of fake trust badges.
content: 3.7 rating and 3 reviews noted in lead data

sections.purpose: Business-specific editorial point of view about Manhattan HVAC intake.
content: Large statement about context before assumptions
Short explanation of building access, equipment location, and symptoms

sections.purpose: Useful service architecture that does not pretend an explicit service list exists.
Sticky buyer guide for what to prepare before calling.
Honest proof and contact facts using only supplied lead data.
Call-to-next-step flow without timing or outcome promises.
Accessible interaction that explains careful, factual expectations.
content: No invented promises on this page

sections.purpose: High-contrast conversion panel with real contact paths.
content: Start with a direct call headline

sections.purpose: Finished footer with brand, anchors, and factual data.

interactions.name: Request router tabs
interactions.type: React state or accessible button group
interactions.reason: Helps visitors map their HVAC issue to a call context without fake service claims.

interactions.name: Commitments accordion
interactions.reason: Adds useful interaction and keeps decision guidance scannable.

interactions.name: Anchor navigation
interactions.type: Real section IDs with smooth-scroll only when motion is allowed
interactions.reason: Makes the one-page site feel complete and navigable.

interactions.name: Mobile sticky call bar
interactions.type: Fixed bottom CTA on small screens
interactions.reason: Phone exists and is the main conversion path.

email.note: Do not render email CTA because business.email is empty.

rules: Use overflow-x: hidden on the shell
Collapse hero to one column and keep CTA row visible
Give MechanicalMapPanel a stable aspect ratio
Turn positioning strip into a 2x2 grid
Make RequestRouter selector horizontally scroll-safe or stacked
Disable sticky positioning for DecisionGuide on mobile
Show MobileStickyCallBar and add bottom padding to main/footer
Keep all buttons readable and tappable at 44px minimum height

stagePlan: Read data/business.json, data/design.json, data/sections.json, data/site-plan.json, and DESIGN_SPEC.md before implementation.
Replace starter page and CSS from blank canvas while preserving factual JSON data.
Set up next/font/google with Fraunces and Instrument Sans.
Build helpers, content arrays, and full section composition in app/page.tsx.
Write CSS variables, layout grids, responsive rules, focus states, and reduced-motion handling in app/globals.css.
Add interactions for request tabs, accordion, anchors, and mobile call bar.
Run build and visually inspect desktop and mobile for overflow, hierarchy, contrast, and generic-template failure.
qaChecklist: At least 9 meaningful sections plus footer are present
Custom fonts are loaded through next/font/google
No stock images or fabricated business photos are used
No fake awards, licenses, guarantees, emergency availability, same-day claims, warranties, or years in business appear
Rating and reviews are presented neutrally as lead-data context if shown
Hero is art-directed and not a centered generic SaaS layout
Service architecture is not just repeated cards
Mobile has sticky CTA and no horizontal overflow
Focus states and reduced-motion handling exist
Final CTA and footer are complete
The page cannot be rebranded for any other contractor by changing only the name
